reasoning:
The March 1 baseline price is $363.27 (the market close on Friday, Feb 27), which represents a local maximum after a sudden $24 (~7%) jump in the last two days of February. 

For the March 8 resolution, the relevant closing price will be Friday, March 6. L3Harris has a scheduled quarterly dividend of $1.25 to shareholders of record on March 6. Under current T+1 settlement rules, this means the stock will go ex-dividend on March 6, triggering a mechanical price drop of $1.25 (~0.35%) at market open. This immediate headwind, combined with a potential mean-reverting pullback from the recent local high, makes it slightly less likely that the price strictly exceeds $363.27 just five trading days later. We assign a 46% probability.

For the March 31 resolution, approximately 22 trading days will pass. The historical upward random drift of the market (~0.8% per month) is generally enough to neutralize and surpass the $1.25 dividend mechanical drop. As the forecast horizon extends, standard volatility increases, widening the distribution of possible prices. Consequently, the effect of the local high and mechanical drop fade, pushing the probability slightly above a coin-toss (51%). As required, uncertainty expands and the probability moves closer to 50% for the more distant date.
